2605.22544.title: One prompt is not enough: Instruction Sensitivity Undermines Embedding Model Evaluation
2605.22544.paper_summary: Instruction embedding models have become common among state-of-the-art models, however are evaluated using a single prompt per task. The single-point evaluation ignores a main problem of the instruction-based approach namely: sensitivity to the phrasing of the instruction. We present an empirical study of prompt sensitivity across 6 embedding models, 11 datasets, and 15 task-specific prompts per dataset, a total of 990. We show that reported scores misrepresent the distribution of scores over plausible prompts. The default prompt can both systematically understate or overstate performance. Furthermore, we show that the leaderboard ranking is not robust to prompt selection: by choosing prompts favorably, any model in our study can be promoted to first place. Our findings suggest that single-prompt evaluation is insufficient for instruction-tuned embedding models and that benchmarks should incorporate prompt robustness, either by evaluating over multiple prompts or by reporting sensitivity alongside point estimates.
2605.22544.paper_summary_zh: 指令嵌入模型在最先進的模型中已變得相當普遍，然而它們的評估僅使用每個任務的一個提示。這種單點評估忽略了基於指令的方法的一個主要問題，即對指令措辭的敏感性。我們對6個嵌入模型、11個數據集以及每個數據集15個特定任務的提示進行了實證研究，總共990個提示。我們顯示，報告的分數錯誤地表現了合理提示的分數分佈。默認提示可能系統性地低估或高估性能。此外，我們顯示，排行榜排名對提示選擇並不穩健：通過選擇有利的提示，我們研究中的任何模型都可以被提升到第一名。我們的發現表明，單一提示評估對於經過指令調整的嵌入模型來說是不足的，基準應該納入提示的穩健性，無論是通過對多個提示進行評估，還是通過報告敏感性與點估計一起。
2605.22544.author: Yevhen Kostiuk et.al.
